story.title: Dr. Torres Models the Dance of Atoms
story.text:
My name is Dr. Michael Torres. I am a computational chemist. I use powerful computers to model chemical reactions.

My models show how atoms rearrange. We start with ingredients called reactants. The reaction changes them into new substances called products. Sometimes it looks like things disappear. A log burns and becomes a small pile of ash. But the atoms did not disappear! They just rearranged to become invisible gases.

This is the law of conservation of mass. The total mass always stays the same. Atoms are not created or destroyed. My job is to predict the reaction rate. This is how fast reactants turn into products. I can change things in my computer model. Higher temperature makes atoms move faster. This speeds up the reaction. More reactants also make the reaction faster. As reactants get used up, the reaction slows down. We want to make useful products safely and quickly. My models help us do that.
think_abouts: Why does Dr. Torres say a burning log doesn't really 'disappear'?
What are two things Dr. Torres can change in his model to make a reaction go faster?
story.go_deeper: Dr. Torres sometimes adds a 'catalyst' to his models. A catalyst makes a reaction 1,000 times faster but doesn't get used up. How might this be useful for making things like medicine?

margin_defs.definition: A computer simulation or copy of a real process.
To move into new positions or patterns.

check_prompts: True or False: In a chemical reaction, the total mass goes down as reactants are used up.

vocabulary.definition: A starting substance in a chemical reaction that gets changed.
vocabulary.use_sentence: In the experiment, vinegar and baking soda were the reactants.

vocabulary.definition: A new substance that is formed by a chemical reaction.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The bubbles of gas were a product of the reaction.

vocabulary.definition: The scientific law that says mass is never created or destroyed in a reaction.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Because of conservation of mass, the total weight was the same before and after mixing.

vocabulary.definition: The speed of a chemical reaction.
vocabulary.use_sentence: We added heat to increase the reaction rate and make it go faster.

vocabulary.definition: A way to write out a chemical reaction using symbols.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The chemical equation showed how H₂ and O₂ combine to make H₂O.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Torres models reactions to make useful products. What is a product you use every day that is made through a chemical reaction?
discussion_cards.frame: A product I use that is made from a chemical reaction is ___ because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: If you wanted to slow down a chemical reaction, like food spoiling in the kitchen, what is one thing you could change based on the reading?
discussion_cards.frame: To slow down a reaction, I could change the ___ by making it lower.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
